title: Fiery Molten Indian Kidney Beans Rajma
description: A spicy and sour Indian dish featuring kidney beans in a molten sauce, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This recipe incorporates suet and dehydrated ingredients for a unique flavor and texture.
instructions: Rinse the dried kidney beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and boil the kidney beans in 4 cups of water until they are tender.
Melt the suet in a pan over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds to the melted suet and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Chop the onion, tomato, ginger, and garlic, and add them to the pan.
Saute the mixture until the vegetables are soft and lightly browned.
Add the cayenne pepper, salt, and boiled kidney beans to the pan, and stir well.
Reduce heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice and let the mixture simmer for another 2 minutes.
Serve the Fiery Molten Indian Kidney Beans Rajma hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
